title: Amazon S3 now requires virtual-hosted-style requests
raw_changelog: Amazon S3 will stop supporting path-style API requests on September 30, 2026. All requests must use virtual-hosted-style.
before_text:
## S3 Request Styles

### Path Style (supported)
https://s3.amazonaws.com/bucket-name/key-name

### Virtual Hosted Style (supported)
https://bucket-name.s3.amazonaws.com/key-name

Both styles are supported for all S3 operations.
after_text:
## S3 Request Styles

### Path Style (DEPRECATED - removed September 30, 2026)
https://s3.amazonaws.com/bucket-name/key-name

### Virtual Hosted Style (required)
https://bucket-name.s3.amazonaws.com/key-name

**Breaking Change:** Path-style requests will return 403 Forbidden after September 30, 2026. Migrate to virtual-hosted-style requests.
diff_text:
--- before
+++ after
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@

-### Path Style (supported)
+### Path Style (DEPRECATED - removed September 30, 2026)

-### Virtual Hosted Style (supported)
+### Virtual Hosted Style (required)

+**Breaking Change:** Path-style requests will return 403 Forbidden after September 30, 2026.

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Path-style S3 requests will stop working. Applications using path-style URLs will receive 403 errors.
ground_truth.action_required: Migrate all S3 requests to virtual-hosted-style URLs. Update AWS SDK to latest version. Deadline: September 30, 2026.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Major breaking change with clear deadline
